Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria), with low serum glucose and HbA1c, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, bilirubin). These findings suggest possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease, with glycosuria and ketonuria possibly indicating underlying metabolic disturbance. Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended.
